text: CheXNet (Rajpurkar et al. 2017, Stanford) demonstrated that a 121-layer DenseNet could detect pneumonia from chest X-rays at a level exceeding practicing radiologists, catalyzing AI adoption in medical imaging.
DeepMind's AI system for breast cancer screening (McKinney et al. 2020, Nature) reduced false positives by 5.7% and false negatives by 9.4% compared to human radiologists in UK and US datasets.
The FDA has cleared over 770 AI medical devices primarily for radiology as of 2025, with deep learning-based imaging tools representing the largest category of AI-enabled healthcare products.
